chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, in this video I'm going to talk about row major and column major representation

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: or mapping.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here we will learn how compilers manage arrays, either single dimension or two dimensional

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: or three dimension.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How they manage their elements, what the machine code they write when we use arrays in our

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: program.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have declared an array of size 5.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In our program when we declare any array, then at run time we get the array space of

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: specified size.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here the indices are starting from 0 to 4.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As a C language example if we take, this is called as base address or this is also called

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: as location 0 or L0.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So L0 here is 100.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In our program if we write a of 2 assign 15.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here is 15.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: When we write this statement, compiler will convert this into machine code.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for storing the value 15 at this particular location, it needs the address of that location

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and that address of that location is 104 as per our example.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here it should be replaced with 104.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see how compiler will make it as 104.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So when we use any index or any location of an array when we access, compiler will convert

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: it into a formula which will get the address of that location at run time.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see how it will write on the formula.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Address of a of 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This address cannot be absolute.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It will generate a formula for getting relative address.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for relative address, the base address is taken.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: L0 plus the index what we have written 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then into the size of the data type.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here we have taken integer as a data type.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So integer is 2 bytes.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is size of data type.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So at run time, if the program is getting the memory at location 100, that 100 is becoming

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the base address of an array, then here it will be 100 plus 2 into 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is 104.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's how it gets the address of any location.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For any location a of i, to get the address of any location, compiler will replace it

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: with the formula L0 i into W. And W is word size.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is index and this is word size.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So word size is the size of data type.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If it is float in C language, it will be 4 bytes.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If it is character, then it will be 1 byte.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Depending on the data type, the value of W may change.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is how the formula is written by the compiler.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We write simply a of i or a of 2, anything, but it will be replaced by the formula by

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: using that particular index.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Some languages, prior to C language, before C language, languages used to allow the indices

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: starting from 1 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here, you can see the index is starting from 1 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And in the declaration also, array a starts from 1 and ends at 5.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now in this case, what will be the formula?

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for getting the address of any location, a of i, formula will be L0 plus i minus 1

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: into W.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As per this example, if we get the address of a of 2, then L0 is 100 plus i is 2 minus

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 1 into 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this gives 100 plus 2, that is 102.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So yes, this is 102.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Index of 2 is 102.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the formula used for accessing any location if the indices are starting

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: from 1 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This used to happen in the languages before C language.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: After C language, every language started supporting array indices starting from 0 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The reason is, if you see the formula for indices starting from 0, just now we saw the

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: formula was L0 plus i into W. If you compare with this formula for indices starting from

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 1, then one operation is extra here.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Minus is extra.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So from C language onwards, this extra operation is removed and they force the programmer to

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: declare an array starting from index 0 only.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now before that, C language, some languages used to allow the indices starting from any

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: index ending at any index.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us say even we can declare an array of minus 3 to 5 as some type, let us say integer.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So even this was allowed.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if the language is allowing the indices like this, it depends on the compiler.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If the compiler is allowing it like this, then what will be the formula?

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the address of any location A of i can be obtained by taking L0 plus i minus lower

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: bound into word size.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be the formula for any starting index.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's all for one-dimensional arrays.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us go to two-dimensional arrays.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In two-dimensional array, there are two options.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Either a compiler can follow row measure method or column measure method.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So there are two types of mapping.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have declared an array of size 3 by 4.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we draw using pen and paper, we draw an array like this with indices so and so.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But in real, the array will be created as a single dimension, single dimension in real

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: because memory is linear and the memory has a single value address, that is integer address.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So actual array will be created like this.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But compiler will allow us to access the array in the two-dimensional form.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I have taken the addresses 500, 502, 4, 6 and in excess this is 8, this is this location.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So these are the addresses I have taken.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now first we will see row measure mapping for this one.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have taken some elements.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have also filled with the elements.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then let us see how these elements are stored in real in the main memory.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: A00 is stored here.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then A01, 02 and 03.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is first row.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is second row.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the elements will be filled row by row in row measure mapping.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So compiler maps this two-dimensional array on the single dimension array.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the elements will look like this.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this is row measure representation.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The elements from this 2D array are mapped on a single dimension array row by row.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is row 0, row 1 and this is row 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us see how the compiler will make use of base address and generate the formula.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For getting the address of any location, A of 2, 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now 2, 1 if you see it is 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here it is 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will be location 0 plus.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Location 0 is this address.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now it has to go to second row.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it should skip two rows.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First row and second row.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it has to skip two rows.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And in each row how many elements are there?

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The dimension of this array is 3 by 4, 3 cross 4.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So each row is having four elements.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 4.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now when you skip two rows, you will be at the beginning of third row.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is row 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now you have to move one element ahead.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this whole thing is multiplied by the size of integer.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this gives 500 plus 9 into 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is how it gets the address.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it means for getting the address of any location, A of i comma j, compiler will write

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the formula L naught i into, if the dimensions are m cross n, then this is n plus j.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is word size.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the row measure formula when the indices are starting from 0 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If the indices are starting from 1 onwards, then the formula for any location, address

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of any location is written as L naught plus i minus 1 into n plus j minus 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This whole thing multiplied by W. Let us find the address of 3 comma 2 here, 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So address of 3, 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: L naught is 500 plus 3 minus 1 into 4 plus 2 minus 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This whole thing is multiplied by W. And this is 500 plus 8 plus 1, 9 into 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is all about row measure representation.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next we will see column measure mapping or column measure representation.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have taken a two dimensional array and basically the array created in main memory will be single

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: dimension and these will be the addresses.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Assume that address starts from 500 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And I have taken some elements.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And how the elements are filled are A00, A10, A20.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is column 0.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then column 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Column 2 and so on.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the array looks like this.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us see how the column measure formula is written.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you want to get the address of a location that is A of 1 comma 2, 1 comma 2 is at 514.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So address of A of 1 comma 2 is taken as location 0 plus.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now 2, it has to go into second column.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So location 0 is 500.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It's here.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it has to skip two columns.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So two columns.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So as the dimensions are 3 cross 4, that is m cross n, number of elements in each column

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: are 3.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 3 plus.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Once we skip two columns, we will be here in the beginning of third column.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then we have to move only one location.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is what I.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this whole thing multiplied by 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 500 plus 7 into 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is 514.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you make it as a formula, formula for finding the address of any location, i and j, is L0

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: plus j into m plus i multiplied by W. That's it.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the formula for column major mapping.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In some languages, array indices start from 1 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what will be the formula?

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Address of any location, a of i, j will be L0 plus j minus 1 into m plus i minus 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Whole thing multiplied by W.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you want to get the address of any location, let us say a 1 comma 4, that is 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us get the address by putting the values in the formula.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Address of 1, 4.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is location.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Plus j value is 4.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 4 minus 1 into, this is 3 cross 4, that is m cross n.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So m value is 3.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 3 plus 1 minus 1.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is integer type.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you assume that this is integer type, then this is 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 500 plus 9 into 2.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this is 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We got the address 518.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now finally, let us look at the formula for row major and column major mapping if the

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: array indices are starting from 0 onwards.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Both the formulas are given here.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: L0, L0 plus i into n plus j into W. And here j into m plus i into W. If you see the operations,

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is having 1, 2, 2 addition operations and 2 multiplications.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here also 2 additions and 2 multiplications.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we can say that both the formulas are equally efficient.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Both are equally efficient.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So a compiler can use any one of the formula.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if we are designing our own compiler, we are designing our, we are developing our own

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: language, then we can use any one of these formulas.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But the C language and all other languages that belongs to C family, they use row major

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: mapping.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's all.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Thank you for watching the video.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Please leave comments so that I can improve the videos based on your feedback.

chunks.title: and Column-Major Mapping - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Thank you for watching.

text: Hi, in this video I'm going to talk about row major and column major representation or mapping. Here we will learn how compilers manage arrays, either single dimension or two dimensional or three dimension. How they manage their elements, what the machine code they write when we use arrays in our program. So let us see. Here I have declared an array of size 5. In our program when we declare any array, then at run time we get the array space of specified size. Here the indices are starting from 0 to 4. As a C language example if we take, this is called as base address or this is also called as location 0 or L0. So L0 here is 100. In our program if we write a of 2 assign 15. Here is 15. When we write this statement, compiler will convert this into machine code. So for storing the value 15 at this particular location, it needs the address of that location and that address of that location is 104 as per our example. So here it should be replaced with 104. So let us see how compiler will make it as 104. So when we use any index or any location of an array when we access, compiler will convert it into a formula which will get the address of that location at run time. So let us see how it will write on the formula. Address of a of 2. This address cannot be absolute. It will generate a formula for getting relative address. So for relative address, the base address is taken. L0 plus the index what we have written 2. Then into the size of the data type. Here we have taken integer as a data type. So integer is 2 bytes. So that is 2. So this is size of data type. So at run time, if the program is getting the memory at location 100, that 100 is becoming the base address of an array, then here it will be 100 plus 2 into 2. That is 104. That's how it gets the address of any location. For any location a of i, to get the address of any location, compiler will replace it with the formula L0 i into W. And W is word size. This is index and this is word size. So word size is the size of data type. If it is float in C language, it will be 4 bytes. If it is character, then it will be 1 byte. Depending on the data type, the value of W may change. So this is how the formula is written by the compiler. We write simply a of i or a of 2, anything, but it will be replaced by the formula by using that particular index. Some languages, prior to C language, before C language, languages used to allow the indices starting from 1 onwards. So here, you can see the index is starting from 1 onwards. And in the declaration also, array a starts from 1 and ends at 5. Now in this case, what will be the formula? So for getting the address of any location, a of i, formula will be L0 plus i minus 1 into W. As per this example, if we get the address of a of 2, then L0 is 100 plus i is 2 minus 1 into 2. So this gives 100 plus 2, that is 102. So yes, this is 102. Index of 2 is 102. So this is the formula used for accessing any location if the indices are starting from 1 onwards. This used to happen in the languages before C language. After C language, every language started supporting array indices starting from 0 onwards. The reason is, if you see the formula for indices starting from 0, just now we saw the formula was L0 plus i into W. If you compare with this formula for indices starting from 1, then one operation is extra here. Minus is extra.
